system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  no;

maxCo           1;

maxDeltaT       1;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

}

thermoType
{

mixture

    specie
    {
        molWeight       28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp              1004.5;
        Hf              0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu              0;
        Pr              1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

internalField   uniform (1.3 0 0);

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system', content='/*--------------------------------*- C++ -*----------------------------------*\n\n    setFieldsDict

\n/*--------------------------------*- C++ -*----------------------------------*/'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices
(
    (0    0    -0.05)   // 0

    (3    0    -0.05)   // 8
    (3    0.2  -0.05)   // 9
    (3    0.2   0.05)   // 10
    (3    0     0.05)   // 11
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Lower region (under step, x: 0->0.6, y: 0->0.2)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (25 8 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper region upstream (above step, x: 0.6->3, y: 0->0.2)
    hex (1 8 9 2 5 10 11 6) (100 8 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Full height region downstream (x: 0.6->3, y: 0->1)
    hex (2 9 10 3 6 11 7 8) (100 33 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (8 11 10 9)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {

        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (8 11 10 9)

    top
    {
        type wall;

        (
            (3 7 11 10)

    obstacle

        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (2 9 11 6)
            (6 11 10 7)
            (1 10 11 5)
            (2 11 10 6)
        );
    }
    defaultFaces
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: "Trying to specify a boundary face 4(1 5 6 2) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch."

2. **`log.rhoCentralFoam`**: Cannot find mesh — this is expected since `blockMesh` failed.

## Root Cause Analysis

The error is clear: **face `(1 5 6 2)` is assigned to the `inlet` patch, but this face is an INTERNAL face between Block 0 and Block 1**. In the three-block forward-step mesh, face `(1 5 6 2)` is the interface between the lower block (under the step) and the upstream upper block — it's an internal face, not an external boundary.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- **Block 0**: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — occupies x=0→0.6, y=0→0.2
- **Block 1**: `hex (1 8 9 2 5 10 11 6)` — occupies x=0.6→3, y=0→0.2

The face `(1 5 6 2)` connects vertices:
- 1: (0.6, 0, -0.05)
- 5: (0.6, 0, 0.05)
- 6: (0.6, 0.2, 0.05)
- 2: (0.6, 0.2, -0.05)

This is the **vertical face at x=0.6** separating Block 0 from Block 1. It is an **internal face** and should NOT be assigned to any boundary patch.

## Recommended Fixes for `blockMeshDict`

1. **Remove face `(1 5 6 2)` from the `inlet` patch**. The `inlet` should only contain the face at x=0:
   ```
   inlet
   {
       type patch;
       faces
       (
           (0 4 7 3)
       );
   }
   ```

2. **Add the face `(1 5 6 2)` to the `obstacle` patch**. This vertical face at x=0.6, y=0→0.2 is the upstream face of the step, which should have slip boundary conditions:
   ```
   obstacle
   {
       type patch;
       faces
       (
           (1 2 6 5)
           (2 9 11 6)
           (6 11 10 7)
           (1 10 11 5)
           (2 11 10 6)
           (1 5 6 2)   // ADD THIS FACE
       );
   }
   ```

3. **Alternatively**, if you prefer to keep the current boundary assignment structure, **split Block 0 into two separate blocks** so that the face `(1 5 6 2)` becomes an external boundary. This would mean:
   - **Block 0a**: The region under the step, x=0→0.6, y=0→0.2 — `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`
   - **Block 0b**: The region upstream above the step, x=0→0.6, y=0.2→1 — `hex (0 3 7 2 4 5 11 10)` — but this mixes x-coordinates incorrectly.

The **cleaner and recommended approach** is option 1: simply remove `(1 5 6 2)` from `inlet` and add it to `obstacle`. This preserves the three-block structure and correctly assigns the internal face to the obstacle patch where it belongs (since it's part of the step surface).

## Summary

The sole fix needed in `blockMeshDict`:
- **Remove face `(1 5 6 2)` from the `inlet` patch definition** — this face is internal between Block 0 and Block 1.
- **Add face `(1 5 6 2)` to the `obstacle` patch** — this is the upstream face of the forward-facing step and should have slip BCs.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Remove face (1 5 6 2) from the inlet patch faces list, keeping only face (0 4 7 3) in inlet; Add face (1 5 6 2) to the obstacle patch faces list, placing it after the existing five faces'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 1.3m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
